THE GREEHEY FAMILY FOUNDATION

Foundation News

Rooted in Compassion. Driven by Purpose.
Inaugural IssueFall 2025
A Letter from Our Chair

Giving with Grace

Since 2004, The Greehey Family Foundation has served as a significant philanthropic force in San Antonio. The Foundation was created by Mom and Dad (Gran and Papa) as a way to deepen and enhance our individual commitments to the community as each of us work, volunteer, and make personal contributions to organizations that make a positive impact.

From the beginning, their intent was that current and future generations of our family will care deeply about the world around them, and that they will actively engage with and give back to our communities.

It is my hope that a bi-annual newsletter will help keep our ever-growing family apprised of the work of the Foundation, and provide helpful insight and information related to governance, stewardship, and our nonprofit partners.

Lisa Rosenbloom
Chair & President

This newsletter is intended for the Greehey Family members only. Please do not circulate.

“You are never truly a success until you share your success with others.”
greeheyfoundation.org
Our Mission

The Greehey Family Foundation is committed to transforming the lives of under-served populations in San Antonio and Bexar County — providing grants to effective nonprofit organizations dedicated to improving the health and human welfare of children and youth, families, the elderly, the homeless, and other disadvantaged individuals.

Putting People First

We prioritize the well-being and dignity of every individual.

Community Commitment

We are dedicated to strengthening and uplifting the community.

Integrity & Fairness

Trust earned through fairness, transparency, and respect.

Foundation News · Fall 2025 · 1
Who We Are

Mission. Vision. Values.

Founded by William and Louree Greehey, the Foundation represents a lasting family legacy of giving back. Our family strives to stay meaningfully involved in supporting nonprofit organizations that strengthen our community and lift up our neighbors.

The Foundation’s charitable giving focuses on San Antonio and Bexar County — a community where our family roots are deep and our commitment to philanthropy continues to grow.

A Guiding Belief

“You are never truly a success until you share your success with others.”

The Values That Guide Us

Putting People First

We prioritize the well-being and dignity of every individual we serve.

Community Commitment

We are dedicated to strengthening and uplifting the community we call home.

Integrity & Fairness

We earn and maintain trust through fairness, transparency, and respect in all that we do.

A Family Legacy

Created in 2004 so that current and future generations of our family will care deeply about the world around them and give back.

Our Focus

We transform the lives of under-served populations by funding effective nonprofits that improve the health and human welfare of children and youth, families, the elderly, the homeless, and other disadvantaged individuals across San Antonio and Bexar County.

Foundation News · Fall 2025 · 2
Doing This Work Well

Governance & Stewardship

Recently, Erin Hogan, our philanthropy consultant with Bank of America, and Suzie Feldmann, our Foundation’s Interim Executive Director, led a Governance Workshop for the board to review the roles and duties of board members.

Discussion Topics

Fiduciary Duties

The Duty of Loyalty (placing the Foundation’s interests above personal interests), the Duty of Care (diligent preparation and active participation), and the Duty of Obedience (following all applicable laws and regulations).

Self-Dealing & Prohibited Transactions

We are developing formal policies to ensure compliance with the law and to promote fairness in our grantmaking and business decisions.

Financial Management

The Foundation must distribute at least 5% of its assets annually. Qualified distributions include grants as well as necessary and reasonable administrative expenses.

Succession Planning

New bylaws create the opportunity to expand our board to include “next-gen” family members beginning in 2026. Candidates must have reached age thirty and be ready to commit to three, three-year terms.

From the Board Orientation Manual

In a family foundation, the board of directors is responsible for governance and oversight of all foundation activities — determining the overall mission, vision, and strategy, and making all funding and policy decisions.

Members of the board have a fiduciary responsibility to provide proper financial oversight, avoid conflicts of interest, and ensure adequate resources for the foundation to fulfill its mission.

Foundation News · Fall 2025 · 3
November 12, 2025

Fall 2025 Board Meeting

On November 12, the Foundation Board held our Annual Meeting to conduct necessary business and to review more than 140 grant requests.

Meeting Highlights

Financial Overview

Presented by our accountant Stephanie Bostick of Forvis Mazars. The Foundation’s current assets are valued at $250 million — approximately the seventh largest foundation in San Antonio, placing it in the top 3% nationally (fewer than 3% of foundations hold more than $50 million in assets).

Bylaw Amendments

Robin Ferriby, an attorney with Clark Hill, explained amendments to the bylaws. Bill and Louree retain their founding-director status, with titles changing to Chair Emeritus and Vice-Chair Emeritus. Lisa steps into the role of Chair and President in anticipation of hiring a full-time chief executive.

Community Needs Assessment

Suzie Feldmann presented a brief assessment highlighting critical community needs arising from the cessation of SNAP benefits, the government shutdown, and federal funding cuts — data that proved helpful as the board reviewed grant requests.

$250M
Foundation Assets
140+
Grant Requests Reviewed
Top 3%
Of Foundations Nationally
Foundation News · Fall 2025 · 4
Putting Generosity to Work

2025 Grants

At the Fall Board Meeting, we decided to grant more than $7 million to local nonprofit organizations. Combined with the grants awarded at the June Board Meeting, our 2025 grand total reached $13,279,000 in critical funding for nonprofit organizations in Bexar County.

Selected Grants

Witte Museum — $3,000,000

A grant over two years to support their endowment campaign.

Madonna Center — $350,000

Supporting the transformation of their Children’s Campus.

Meals on Wheels — $150,000

Ensuring nutritious meals are delivered to homebound seniors.

Early Matters San Antonio — $100,000

Supporting high-quality early learning and childhood development.

Children’s Bereavement Center — $100,000

Providing free grief counseling for children and families.

San Antonio Food Bank — $100,000

Supporting food distributions to vulnerable families.

Meeting the Moment

Many grants were intended to address critical community needs stemming from federal funding cuts, the cessation of SNAP, and the government shutdown. Bexar County has more than 300,000 individuals who rely on SNAP, and approximately 189,000 federal workers and government contractors were furloughed.

Looking Ahead

Policies & Procedures

Reflecting our commitment to good governance, we are developing official policies covering Conflicts of Interest, Records Retention, Whistleblower Protocols, Investment Guidelines, and Compensation and Expenses.

Executive Search

We have engaged Denise Smith Consulting to help us identify and hire our first full-time President in 2026, who will report to the Chair and Board and lead day-to-day operations. We will then turn to expanding the board with “next-gen” members.

Website Refresh

We have engaged Trade-Craft, a creative agency, to refresh our website ahead of the executive search. A sneak peek is coming soon.

IT Services

We have engaged Documation to provide our IT services, including network monitoring, backups, email management, and cyber security.

Thank you for being part of this family legacy.

The Greehey Family Foundation
Rooted in Compassion · Driven by Purpose
